channel.title: Least-Privilege on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Least-Privilege on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: security: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
item.description:
Microsoft Security published a post on applying least-privilege principles to AI agents, focusing on identity, access control, tool binding, and auditing as agents become more autonomous.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Improving security posture across the Microsoft partner ecosystem
item.description:
Microsoft says it is improving security across its partner ecosystem by tightening CSP vetting, applying least-privilege access, increasing monitoring, and promoting risk-management best practices. The post is framed as guidance for partners rather than a product release.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Read-only Actions cache for untrusted triggers
item.description:
GitHub Actions now uses read-only cache tokens on the default branch for workflow events that can be triggered without repository write permissions. This narrows cache access for untrusted triggers by applying least privilege.

  • Source: GitHub
